purpose: Python 3 bootstrapper that downloads and stages the pinned native build dependencies into repo-local directories so the CMake build is fully offline-reproducible. Fetches Microsoft.Web.WebView2 1.0.3912.50 (NuGet nupkg, extracted to packages/Microsoft.Web.WebView2.<ver>), the latest nuget.exe to tools/nuget/, cmark-gfm 0.29.0.gfm.13 (GitHub tarball, extracted to third_party/cmark-gfm with a CMake-floor patch), and Mermaid 11.14.0 (npm tarball, extracts package/dist/mermaid.min.js to MarkdownPlusPlus.Native/assets/mermaid/mermaid.min.js). Uses a .download-cache directory for intermediate artifacts which it deletes on success.
calls_out_to: fs/file IO: packages/, tools/nuget/, third_party/cmark-gfm/, MarkdownPlusPlus.Native/assets/mermaid/, .download-cache/
invariants: Writes ONLY under the repository root - per the module docstring contract. No user/system locations are touched.
Idempotent: download() short-circuits when the destination file exists and is non-empty; install_webview2 returns if the extracted package_dir already exists.
install_cmark_gfm strips the top-level prefix from the tarball so files land directly under third_party/cmark-gfm; existing third_party/cmark-gfm is deleted first to avoid stale-file pollution.
patch_cmark_gfm_cmake raises cmark-gfm's cmake_minimum_required from 3.0 / 3.5 to 3.10 so CMake 4+ does not error on compatibility floor.
gotchas: Bumping any of the three version constants requires verifying: (a) the WebView2 SDK still contains x64/WebView2LoaderStatic.lib and include/WebView2.h, (b) cmark-gfm still uses the same library targets (libcmark-gfm_static, libcmark-gfm-extensions_static), and (c) Mermaid still publishes package/dist/mermaid.min.js in the tarball.
The Mermaid tarball entry path 'package/dist/mermaid.min.js' is checked literally - Mermaid renaming the dist path would silently fail.
Uses urllib without certificate-pinning and follows redirects implicitly; assumes corporate proxies/MITM proxies are configured at the OS level.
